suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify mors-global-worker skill step 8 (manual CLI/API/SSE sanity checks): workers should either (a) perform and report manual checks for user-facing CLI/API changes, or (b) explicitly state N/A with justification when the feature is library-level or the surface is not manually testable in the dev environment. Workers must set followedProcedure=false when skipping required manual steps without N/A justification.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 4 fix features in round 2 (relay-foundation-fix-host-binding-and-persistence-bootstrap, relay-foundation-fix-cli-auth-regating-and-token-liveness, relay-foundation-fix-relay-auth-wiring-fail-closed, relay-foundation-fix-e2ee-bootstrap-runtime-integration) marked followedProcedure=true but reported only automated commandsRun with no manual/interactive check evidence. Same pattern observed in round 1 (4/5 original features).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to mors-global-worker skill a testing caveat: when writing CLI integration tests that use spawnSync/execSync against commands that start an in-process mock server, the synchronous child process blocks the Node.js event loop, preventing the mock server from responding. Use either (a) a separate background server process, (b) async child_process.exec with proper cleanup, or (c) pure unit tests with mocked I/O for such scenarios.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Worker session 23898abf-e853-4d6a-a0be-8999d5600754 (relay-foundation-fix-cli-auth-regating-and-token-liveness) discovered and documented this pitfall in test/auth/cli-auth-gating.test.ts:106-109 and in handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges. The constraint is not currently documented in the skill.
